sourcePrompt: An M151 MUTT, the US quarter-ton utility jeep, about three and a half meters long with all four tires on the ground. A small open-topped jeep: a flat windshield folded upright above a flat grille with two round headlights, an open two-seat body with simple bucket seats and a flat hood, big square wheel arches over knobby tires, and a spare tire bolted on the flat tail. Olive-drab steel, boxy, light and rugged.
handEdit: steering wheel disc reoriented to face driver perpendicular to column (torus default faced sideways; old z-spin was a no-op on a torus); offline rebake
